zh.lifeSummary: Agnes Llewellyn de Kingston是William Llewellyn de Kingston的女兒，於1407年出生在英國薩默塞特。她於1430年與Sir Knt John de Maunsell在威爾士格拉莫爾根奧可維其結婚。他們至少是6個兒子的父母。她去世於1476年享年69歲。

nameMeanings.name: Llewellyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Llywelyn, derived from British Celtic Lugubelinos, itself from the divine name Lugus + an uncertain second element often used alone as a name. Through association with llew ‘lion’, it was altered to Llewelyn. This was the name of two princes of north Wales who held the Normans at bay for many years in the 13th century. Etymologically speaking, the form with a fourth -l- is an error, not found as an original Welsh name, but this is now much the most common spelling in the US.

variants.name: Welling
meanings: English: habitational name from Welwyn (Hertfordshire) or Welling in Bexley (Kent), with a suspicion of an alternative unidentified source. The Hertfordshire placename means ‘at the willow trees’, from Old English wel(i)gum, dative plural form of welig ‘willow’. The Kent placename may derive from Old English wella ‘well, spring, stream’ + the placename forming suffix -ing. In some cases this name may represent a development of Llewellyn , particularly near the Welsh border.
German: habitational name from any of various places in the Rhineland called Welling or Wellingen.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Agnes Llewellyn de Kingston was born in 1407, in Somerset, England as the daughter of William Llewellyn de Kingston. She married Sir Knt John de Maunsell in 1430, in Oxwich, Glamorgan, Wales. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. She died in 1476, at the age of 69.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Llewellyn de Kingstonは1407年にイギリスソメルセトでWilliam Llewellyn de Kingstonの娘として生まれた。1430年にウェールズグラモーガンオクスウィチでSir Knt John de Maunsellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子の親であった。彼女は1476年に69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Llewellyn de Kingston(은)는 1407년 영국 서머세트에서 William Llewellyn de Kingston의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1430년 웨일스 글램르건 스위치에서 Sir Knt John de Maunsell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1476년 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Agnes Llewellyn de Kingston родилась в 1407 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия. Её отца звали William Llewellyn de Kingston. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Knt John de Maunsell в 1430 г. в местности оксвич, глэморган, Уэльс. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей. Она умерла в 1476 г. в возрасте 69 лет.
